Hi Murielle. Before the review, how does Australia compare to Europe on turnover?
Good news. Our turnover is lower than Europe. In fact, it is the lowest rate in the company.
Excellent. And what about cost per hire?
Honestly, our cost is higher than the European benchmark. Europe is still the most cost-effective region.
So where is the biggest gap between us?
The biggest gap is cost. But our process is more efficient than last year, and the trend is positive.
Good. So what is your headline for the board?
We are more efficient than last year, we outperform the benchmark on retention, but we can learn from Europe on cost.
Our process is more efficient than last year.
The trend is positive and retention is better than before.

Hi Murielle, it's Liam. Let me compare our two regions before the review, so the numbers are clear. First, our turnover in Australia is lower than in the other regions. It is nine percent, which is the lowest rate in the company. Second, our hiring is faster than last year. The time to fill a role is shorter now, about three weeks. However, I have to be honest: our cost per hire is higher than the European benchmark. It is more expensive because the market is smaller and more competitive. The good news is that retention is better than before, and the trend is positive. In one line: we are more efficient than last year, but Europe is still the most cost-effective region. Let's talk about how to close that gap. Talk soon.

Good morning, Murielle. This is Diana from London, with a quick ranking before the board meeting. I looked at the metrics from all five regions. On engagement, Australia has the highest score, which is excellent. On cost, Europe is the most cost-effective, so we should learn from them. The biggest gap is in onboarding time: some regions are much faster than others. Our best region takes two weeks, but our slowest region takes six. That difference is too large. My recommendation is simple: the fastest region is going to share its process, and every other region is going to follow the same benchmark. If we do this, next year is going to be our strongest year for talent. I am sending you the full ranking now. Thank you.
